_doc: Example .dangeresque/config.json — copy to config.json and edit. All fields are optional; sensible defaults apply when omitted.
_doc_verify: Pre-review verification: dangeresque runs each command in the worktree between the worker exit (post-rebase, post-file-count-normalize) and the review pass. Catches drift between worker prose claims ("yarn build passes") and code reality. Empty `commands` array (default) means no-op; opt in by listing commands. The CLI runs them, NOT the engine — `allowedTools` does not constrain them.

verify._doc_modes: Modes that trigger verification. INVESTIGATE is excluded by default since it produces no code changes.
verify._doc_commands: Per-command policy: name (unique id used in artifact + SUMMARY), cmd (shell string), on_failure ('block' skips review and fails the run; 'warn' continues), timeout_ms (defaults to 300000 = 5min).

_doc_scope: Scope subsystem: project-level opportunistic-fix budget. `enabled` toggles file/line caps (denyGlobs apply unconditionally regardless). `denyGlobs` is the project no-fly list — files matching are demoted to `outside` even if a worker categorized them `extension`; security-sensitive paths cannot be laundered. `maxFiles` caps how many opportunistic drive-by files a worker may bundle in one run; `maxLines` caps total LOC across them. Excess opportunistic files are demoted to `scope_report.outside`, and the reviewer adjudicates. Tighten for stricter projects; relax for greenfield.
_doc_other_examples: Cargo project: { name: 'compile', cmd: 'cargo build', on_failure: 'block' }, { name: 'test', cmd: 'cargo test', on_failure: 'block' }
Go project:    { name: 'vet', cmd: 'go vet ./...', on_failure: 'block' }, { name: 'test', cmd: 'go test ./...', on_failure: 'block' }
TypeScript-only type check: { name: 'typecheck', cmd: 'tsc --noEmit', on_failure: 'block' }
Operator escape hatch: pass --no-verify to skip for one run; or set verify.enabled=false in config; or drop the offending command from `commands`.
